message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Rearden Demo App — Implementation Plan

## Context
Current state: bare Turborepo skeleton with placeholder web app and minimal API. Goal: build a polished demo of a video-first hiring platform focused on the recruiter search experience (investor-facing).

## Decisions
| Decision | Choice |
|----------|--------|
| AI Search | Smart mock — fuzzy scoring + 500ms delay, no Claude API |
| Videos | Real upload via API + Pexels CDN URLs for mock candidates |
| Routing | react-router-dom v7 with 4 pages |
| New deps | `react-router-dom` (web only) |
| Theme | Dark, premium, glass morphism, purple/teal accents |
| Font | Inter via Google Fonts |

## Target Pages
1. `/` — Landing (hero + CTAs + feature cards)
2. `/register` — Candidate registration + video upload
3. `/candidate/:id` — Public profile with video player
4. `/search` — **MAIN PAGE** — recruiter AI search with candidate cards

## File Structure

### `packages/types/src/` (modify)
- `candidate.ts` — add: videoUrl, thumbnailUrl, location, title, bio, availability
- `search.ts` — NEW: SearchQuery, SearchFilters, SearchResult, SearchResponse
- `index.ts` — add search exports

### `apps/api/src/` (add)
```
routes/
  candidates.ts    GET /, GET /:id, POST /
  search.ts        POST / (fuzzy scoring + delay)
  upload.ts        POST /upload, GET /uploads/:filename
data/
  mockCandidates.ts  12 diverse candidates with Pexels video URLs
lib/
  searchEngine.ts    Weighted fuzzy scoring algorithm
```

### `apps/web/src/` (rewrite)
```
router.tsx
main.tsx (RouterProvider)
styles/
  global.scss        Dark theme reset
  _variables.scss    Colors, spacing, breakpoints
  _mixins.scss       Media queries, glass morphism
  _animations.scss   Shimmer, pulse, glow keyframes
pages/
  Landing/           Hero, CTAs, features
  Search/            SearchBar + CandidateCard grid (MAIN)
  CandidateProfile/  Video player + info
  Register/          Form + VideoUpload
components/
  Layout/            Navbar + AnimatePresence + Outlet
  Navbar/            Logo, links, active indicator (layoutId)
  VideoPlayer/       <video> with custom controls
  VideoUpload/       Drag-drop + upload progress
  CandidateCard/     Thumbnail, skills, score, hover preview
  SearchBar/         Glowing animated input
  SkillTag/          Pill badge
  ScoreBadge/        Match percentage indicator
  PageTransition/    Motion enter/exit wrapper
  Button/            Primary/secondary/ghost variants
  Input/             Styled form input
  Logo/              Wordmark
hooks/
  useSearch.ts       Debounced search API + state
  useApi.ts          Generic fetch wrapper
  useVideoUpload.ts  Upload progress + preview
lib/
  api.ts             Base URL + typed fetch
```

## API Endpoints
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /api/candidates | List all candidates |
| GET | /api/candidates/:id | Single candidate |
| POST | /api/candidates | Create (registration) |
| POST | /api/search | AI search (fuzzy + 500ms delay) |
| POST | /api/upload | Video upload (multipart) |
| GET | /api/uploads/:filename | Serve uploaded video |

## Search Algorithm
Parse query → extract skills, location, experience → score each candidate:
- Skill exact match: +25pts, partial: +15pts
- Title match: +20pts
- Location match: +15pts
- Bio match: +5pts
- Experience bonus: +10pts
- Normalize to 0-100, return sorted + matchReason string
- Empty query → all candidates as "Featured Talent"

## Animation Strategy (Motion v12, `"motion/react"`)
- **Page transitions**: AnimatePresence mode="wait", fade+slide Y
- **Card grid**: staggerChildren 0.06s, fade+scale+Y
- **Hover**: cards lift (y: -4) + shadow, video scale 1.05
- **SearchBar**: CSS glow border, rotating placeholder crossfade
- **Navbar**: layoutId shared layout for active indicator
- **Landing hero**: spring heading, stagger CTAs, whileInView features

## Design Tokens (dark theme)
- bg: `#0a0a0f` / `#12121a` / `#1a1a2e`
- text: `#f0f0f5` / `#8888a0`
- accent: `#6c5ce7` (purple) / `#00cec9` (teal)
- glass: `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)`, `rgba(255,255,255,0.03)`
- radius: 6/10/16/24px
- shadows: colored tints, not gray

## Execution Order

### Phase 1 — Foundation
1. Extend types (candidate fields + search types)
2. `npm install react-router-dom` in web
3. Vite proxy `/api` → localhost:3001
4. SCSS design system (_variables, _mixins, _animations, global dark theme)
5. Add Inter font to index.html

### Phase 2 — API Backend
1. Mock data (12 candidates + Pexels video URLs)
2. Candidate routes (list, get, create)
3. Upload routes (save to uploads/, serve files)
4. Search engine (fuzzy scoring)
5. Search route, wire all in app.ts

### Phase 3 — Web Core
1. Base components (Button, Input, SkillTag, Logo, ScoreBadge, PageTransition)
2. Layout + Navbar + Router + main.tsx rewrite
3. API client + hooks (useApi, useSearch, useVideoUpload)

### Phase 4 — Pages (in order)
1. Landing page
2. **Search page** (main focus — SearchBar, CandidateCard, grid, animations)
3. Candidate profile (VideoPlayer + info)
4. Register (form + VideoUpload)

### Phase 5 — Polish
1. Responsive (1/2/3 column grid)
2. Animation tuning
3. Loading/error/empty states
4. Lazy loading + video preload="metadata"

## Verification
1. `npm run check-types` — all workspaces pass
2. `npm run build` — both apps build
3. `npm run dev` — web :3000, api :3001
4. Test: visit `/search`, type queries, verify cards + scores
5. Test: click card → `/candidate/:id` with video
6. Test: `/register` → upload video → redirect to profile
7. Test: mobile viewport on all pages


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\yakik\.claude\projects\C--Users-yakik-Documents-projects-rearden\a29f27d8-9b0e-405f-bf58-19e795d0b0b9.jsonl

content.text:
Excellent! Now let me compile a comprehensive summary of the project structure:

## Rearden Project State - Complete Overview

I've thoroughly explored the Rearden project. Here's what exists:

### 1. **packages/types/src/** Files:
- `index.ts` - Main export barrel file
- `user.ts` - Base User interface (id, email, name, role, createdAt, updatedAt)
- `candidate.ts` - Extends User with role="candidate", plus resume, skills, experience
- `recruiter.ts` - Extends User with role="recruiter", plus company, position
- `api-response.ts` - Generic ApiResponse<T> wrapper with success, data, error, meta

### 2. **apps/api/src/** Files:
- `index.ts` - Server entry point using @hono/node-server, runs on port 3001
- `app.ts` - Hono app definition with logger and CORS middleware, two routes: "/" and "/health"
- `generated/prisma/` - Auto-generated Prisma client files (browser.ts, client.ts, models, enums, etc.)

### 3. **apps/web/src/** Files:
- `main.tsx` - React entry point using createRoot
- `App.tsx` - Main component showing demo with Motion animations
- `App.module.scss` - Styled container with flexbox layout
- `styles/global.scss` - Global reset and body styles

### 4. **apps/web/package.json:**
```
Name: web
Dependencies: react 19.1.0, react-dom 19.1.0, motion 12.12.1, @rearden/types
DevDeps: vite 6.3.5, @vitejs/plugin-react 4.5.2, sass 1.87.0, typescript 5.9.2, @rearden/tsconfig
Scripts: dev (vite), build (tsc -b && vite build), preview, check-types
```

### 5. **apps/api/package.json:**
```
Name: api
Dependencies: hono 4.7.10, @hono/node-server 1.14.1, @prisma/client 7.0.0, dotenv 16.5.0, @rearden/types
DevDeps: tsx 4.19.4, typescript 5.9.2, @rearden/tsconfig, prisma 7.0.0, @types/node 22.15.21
Scripts: dev (tsx watch src/index.ts), build (tsc), start (node dist/index.js), check-types
```

### 6. **Root package.json:**
```
Private workspace with workspaces: apps/*, packages/*
Scripts: dev, build, format, check-types (all via turbo)
DevDeps: turbo 2.8.20, typescript 5.9.2, prettier 3.7.4
Node: >=18, npm 11.6.2
```

### 7. **apps/web/vite.config.ts:**
- React plugin enabled
- Path alias "@" → "./src"
- CSS modules with camelCaseOnly convention
- Server port: 3000

### 8. **apps/web/index.html:**
- Standard HTML5 with viewport meta
- Title: "Rearden"
- Root div and module script loading /src/main.tsx

### 9. **apps/api/src/app.ts:**
- Hono instance with logger and CORS middleware
- GET "/" → returns {success: true, data: {message: "Rearden API"}}
- GET "/health" → returns {success: true, data: {status: "ok", timestamp}}

### 10. **apps/web/src/main.tsx:**
- StrictMode wrapper
- Creates React root and renders App
- Imports global styles

### 11. **turbo.json:**
```json
{
  "ui": "tui",
  "tasks": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "inputs": ["$TURBO_DEFAULT$", ".env*"],
      "outputs": [".next/**", "!.next/cache/**", "dist/**"]
    },
    "check-types": {
      "dependsOn": ["^check-types"]
    },
    "dev": {
      "cache": false,
      "persistent": true
    }
  }
}
```

### Key Configuration Files Summary:

**TypeScript Config Structure:**
- `@rearden/tsconfig/base.json` - Strict ES2022 with declaration maps
- `@rearden/tsconfig/vite-react.json` - Extends base + JSX with DOM libs
- `@rearden/tsconfig/node.json` - Extends base + NodeNext module resolution
- `apps/web/tsconfig.json` - Extends vite-react with "@/*" path alias
- `apps/api/tsconfig.json` - Extends node with src → dist compilation
- `packages/types/tsconfig.json` - Extends base with noEmit

**Package Configuration:**
- `@rearden/types` - Raw TypeScript exports (no build step), uses ".": "./src/index.ts"
- `@rearden/tsconfig` - Shared configuration package

### Current State:
- **Web app** (port 3000): Fully functional Vite+React frontend with demo animation showing a User object
- **API app** (port 3001): Minimal Hono API with health check and root endpoint
- **Types package**: Shared types covering User base interface, Candidate, Recruiter, and ApiResponse wrapper
- **Development setup**: npm workspaces + Turbo + TypeScript 5.9.2 across all packages
- **Git status**: Multiple files marked for deletion (docs app, eslint-config, old ui package), web package.json modified, CLAUDE.md modified

All absolute file paths are documented above for reference.

Build the complete API backend for the Rearden video-first hiring platform. The API app is at `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api`.

## Current State
- Hono v4 app at `apps/api/src/app.ts` with logger + CORS middleware
- Entry point at `apps/api/src/index.ts` running on port 3001
- Types at `@rearden/types` with: Candidate (id, email, name, role, resume, skills, experience, videoUrl, thumbnailUrl, location, title, bio, availability), ApiResponse<T>, SearchQuery, SearchFilters, SearchResult, SearchResponse

## What to Build

### 1. `apps/api/src/data/mockCandidates.ts`
Create 12 diverse mock candidates with realistic data. Use Pexels video URLs for video content. Each candidate needs:
- Unique UUID-style id
- Realistic name, email, title, location, bio
- Real tech skills (3-6 per candidate)
- experience: 1-15 years
- availability: "immediate" | "2weeks" | "1month" | "3months"  
- videoUrl: use Pexels video URLs like `https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/{id}/{filename}` — use these REAL Pexels video file URLs:
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3252118/3252118-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209828/3209828-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3255275/3255275-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065924/4065924-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3251806/3251806-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3196487/3196487-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209298/3209298-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3195440/3195440-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4066905/4066905-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065388/4065388-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209191/3209191-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  - https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065564/4065564-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
- thumbnailUrl: use `https://images.pexels.com/videos/{videoId}/free-video-{videoId}.jpg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=400`
- Export as `const mockCandidates: Candidate[]` with proper typing from @rearden/types

### 2. `apps/api/src/lib/searchEngine.ts`
Fuzzy scoring algorithm:
```
export function searchCandidates(candidates: Candidate[], query: string, filters?: SearchFilters): { candidate: Candidate; score: number; matchReason: string }[]
```
Algorithm:
- Parse query into tokens (lowercase, split by space)
- Score each candidate:
  - Skill exact match: +25pts per skill
  - Skill partial match (includes): +15pts per skill
  - Title match (includes token): +20pts per token
  - Location match (includes token): +15pts per token
  - Bio match (includes token): +5pts per token
  - Experience mention (e.g. "senior" → exp >= 5): +10pts
- Apply filters if provided (skills, location, experienceMin/Max, availability)
- Normalize to 0-100 scale
- Build matchReason string (e.g. "Skills: React, TypeScript | Location: San Francisco")
- Sort descending by score
- Return empty query → all candidates as "Featured Talent" with score 85-100 random
- Filter out candidates with score 0 when query is provided

### 3. `apps/api/src/routes/candidates.ts`
Hono router:
- GET `/` → return all candidates from mockCandidates
- GET `/:id` → return single candidate by id (404 if not found)
- POST `/` → create new candidate (add to in-memory array, return it)

### 4. `apps/api/src/routes/search.ts`
Hono router:
- POST `/` → accepts { query, filters } body, runs searchEngine with 500ms artificial delay, returns SearchResponse with results + enriched candidate data

For the search response, return an object that includes both the search metadata AND the full candidate objects so the frontend doesn't need a second request. Use this shape:
```typescript
{
  success: true,
  data: {
    results: Array<SearchResult & { candidate: Candidate }>,
    query: string,
    totalCandidates: number,
    searchTimeMs: number
  }
}
```

### 5. `apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts`
Hono router:
- POST `/` → accept multipart file upload, save to `apps/api/uploads/` directory, return the filename + URL
- GET `/:filename` → serve the uploaded file with proper content-type

Make sure the uploads directory is created if it doesn't exist.

### 6. Update `apps/api/src/app.ts`
Wire all routes:
```typescript
app.route("/api/candidates", candidateRoutes);
app.route("/api/search", searchRoutes);
app.route("/api/upload", uploadRoutes);
```
Keep existing `/` and `/health` routes.

## Important
- Use `import type { ... } from "@rearden/types"` for type imports
- Every response should use `ApiResponse<T>` wrapper
- The app uses Hono v4 — use `c.json()`, `c.req.json()`, `c.req.param()`
- Write all files to their exact paths
- Make sure all imports are correct
- Do NOT install any additional packages — use built-in Node.js APIs for file operations (fs, path)

input.prompt:
Build the core UI components for the Rearden video-first hiring platform web app. The web app is at `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web`.

## Tech Stack
- React 19 + Vite 6
- SCSS Modules with camelCaseOnly convention (import styles from "./Foo.module.scss", use styles.className)
- Motion v12: `import { motion } from "motion/react"` (NOT framer-motion)
- TypeScript 5.9
- Path alias: `@/` maps to `src/`

## Design System
The project has SCSS variables at `src/styles/_variables.scss`:
- Colors: $bgDeep (#0a0a0f), $bgBase (#12121a), $bgElevated (#1a1a2e), $bgSurface (#22223a)
- Text: $textPrimary (#f0f0f5), $textSecondary (#8888a0), $textMuted (#55556a)
- Accents: $accentPurple (#6c5ce7), $accentTeal (#00cec9)
- Glass: $glassBg, $glassBorder, $glassBlur (12px), + hover variants
- Radii: $radiusSm(6), $radiusMd(10), $radiusLg(16), $radiusXl(24), $radiusFull(9999)
- Shadows: $shadowSm, $shadowMd, $shadowLg, $shadowGlow, $shadowTealGlow
- Font: $fontFamily ("Inter"), various $fontSize* vars
- Transitions: $transitionFast(150ms), $transitionBase(250ms), $transitionSlow(400ms)

Mixins at `src/styles/_mixins.scss`:
- @include glass — glassmorphism background
- @include glassHover — hover state for glass
- @include container — max-width container
- @include truncate($lines) — text truncation
- @include focusRing — focus outline
- @include mobile/tablet/desktop/wide — responsive breakpoints

## Components to Build

Each component = folder with `ComponentName.tsx` + `ComponentName.module.scss`.

### 1. `src/components/Button/Button.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: variant ("primary" | "secondary" | "ghost"), size ("sm" | "md" | "lg"), children, disabled, onClick, type, className, href (renders <a> if provided)
- Primary: purple gradient background, white text, glow on hover
- Secondary: glass background, purple border, purple text
- Ghost: transparent, text only, subtle hover background
- Sizes: sm (32px height, fontSize13), md (40px, fontSize14), lg (48px, fontSize16)
- Use motion.button with whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }} and whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}

### 2. `src/components/Input/Input.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: label?, placeholder, value, onChange, type, error?, icon? (ReactNode), className
- Dark background ($bgElevated), glass border
- Focus: purple border glow animation
- Error state: red border + error message below
- Icon positioned inside left of input
- 44px height, $radiusMd border-radius

### 3. `src/components/SkillTag/SkillTag.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: skill: string, variant? ("default" | "match"), size? ("sm" | "md")
- Small pill badge with glass background
- "match" variant: purple background, brighter text
- sm: fontSize12, px 8, py 2
- md: fontSize13, px 12, py 4

### 4. `src/components/ScoreBadge/ScoreBadge.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: score: number (0-100)
- Circular or pill badge showing match percentage
- Color based on score: >=80 teal, >=60 purple, <60 muted
- Show "{score}% match" text
- Subtle glow for high scores

### 5. `src/components/Logo/Logo.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: size? ("sm" | "md" | "lg")
- Text wordmark "rearden" in lowercase
- Gradient text (purple → teal)
- Font weight 700
- Sizes: sm (fontSize18), md (fontSize24), lg (fontSize32)

### 6. `src/components/PageTransition/PageTransition.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: children
- Motion wrapper for page enter/exit animations
- initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
- animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
- exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -20 }}
- transition={{ duration: 0.3, ease: "easeInOut" }}

### 7. `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.tsx` + `.module.scss`
- Fixed top, full width, glass background, 64px height
- Left: Logo component (links to /)
- Right: nav links (Search, Register) using NavLink from react-router-dom
- Active link has a purple underline indicator using motion layoutId="navIndicator"
- Mobile: horizontal scroll nav links or just show all

### 8. `src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx` + `.module.scss`  
- Renders Navbar at top
- AnimatePresence mode="wait" wrapping <Outlet /> from react-router-dom
- Main content area with padding-top for fixed navbar

### 9. `src/components/SearchBar/SearchBar.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: value: string, onChange: (value: string) => void, onSubmit?: () => void, loading?: boolean
- Large input with search icon (use inline SVG)
- Glass background, larger than regular Input (56px height)
- Animated glowing border (CSS animation glowBorder, defined in _animations.scss)
- Rotating placeholder text using crossfade (cycle through: "Search for React developers...", "Find senior engineers in NYC...", "Looking for ML specialists...", "Discover full-stack talent...")
- Loading state: spinning icon or shimmer
- $radiusXl border-radius

### 10. `src/components/CandidateCard/CandidateCard.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: candidate: Candidate, score?: number, matchReason?: string, onClick?: () => void
- Glass card with thumbnail at top (16:9 aspect ratio)
- Below thumbnail: name, title, location
- Skills as SkillTag pills (max 4 shown)
- ScoreBadge in top-right corner (if score provided)
- matchReason text at bottom in muted color
- Hover: lift (motion y: -4), stronger shadow, video thumbnail scale 1.05
- Click: navigates to candidate profile
- Use motion.div for the card

### 11. `src/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: src: string, poster?: string, autoPlay?: boolean
- HTML5 <video> with custom styled controls
- Play/pause button overlay (centered, large, glass background)
- Progress bar at bottom
- Rounded corners ($radiusLg)
- Responsive (max-width 100%)

### 12. `src/components/VideoUpload/VideoUpload.tsx` + `.module.scss`
Props: onUpload: (file: File) => void, uploading?: boolean, progress?: number, previewUrl?: string
- Drag-and-drop zone with dashed border
- Upload icon and "Drag & drop or click to upload" text
- Accept only video/* files
- During upload: progress bar + percentage
- After upload: video preview with option to re-upload
- Glass background, hover border color change

## Router Setup

### `src/router.tsx`
```tsx
import { createBrowserRouter } from "react-router-dom";
import { Layout } from "@/components/Layout/Layout";
// Lazy load pages

export const router = createBrowserRouter([
  {
    element: <Layout />,
    children: [
      { path: "/", element: <Landing /> },
      { path: "/search", element: <Search /> },
      { path: "/candidate/:id", element: <CandidateProfile /> },
      { path: "/register", element: <Register /> },
    ],
  },
]);
```

### `src/main.tsx`
```tsx
import { StrictMode } from "react";
import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider } from "react-router-dom";
import { router } from "./router";
import "./styles/global.scss";

createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </StrictMode>
);
```

## Hooks to Build

### `src/lib/api.ts`
Base API client:
```typescript
const API_BASE = "/api";

export async function apiFetch<T>(path: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
  const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...options?.headers },
    ...options,
  });
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`API Error: ${res.status}`);
  return res.json();
}
```

### `src/hooks/useApi.ts`
Generic fetch hook with loading/error states:
```typescript
function useApi<T>(path: string): { data: T | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null; refetch: () => void }
```

### `src/hooks/useSearch.ts`
Search hook with debouncing:
```typescript
function useSearch(): { query: string; setQuery: (q: string) => void; results: SearchResultWithCandidate[]; loading: boolean; error: string | null }
```
- Debounce 400ms
- Calls POST /api/search with { query }
- Returns enriched results (SearchResult + full Candidate object)

### `src/hooks/useVideoUpload.ts`
Upload hook:
```typescript
function useVideoUpload(): { upload: (file: File) => Promise<string>; uploading: boolean; progress: number; previewUrl: string | null }
```
- Uses XMLHttpRequest for progress tracking
- Returns uploaded file URL

## Important Rules
- SCSS Modules: import as `import styles from "./Component.module.scss"` — use styles.className (camelCase only)
- Motion v12: `import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react"`
- All motion props: whileHover, whileTap, initial, animate, exit, transition, layoutId
- In SCSS files, use `@use "@/styles/variables" as *;` and `@use "@/styles/mixins" as *;` for accessing design tokens
- Use typed imports: `import type { Candidate } from "@rearden/types"`
- Write ALL files (component tsx + scss + hooks + lib + router + main.tsx)
- Create placeholder page components (just a div with the page name) so the router works. Put them in their page folders:
  - `src/pages/Landing/Landing.tsx`
  - `src/pages/Search/Search.tsx`
  - `src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx`
  - `src/pages/Register/Register.tsx`

## SCSS Module Import Pattern
In each .module.scss file, import variables and mixins like this:
```scss
@use "../../styles/variables" as *;
@use "../../styles/mixins" as *;
```
Use relative paths from the component's location to the styles directory.

input.prompt:
Build the complete API backend for the Rearden video-first hiring platform. The API app is at `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api`.

## Current State
- Hono v4 app at `apps/api/src/app.ts` with logger + CORS middleware
- Entry point at `apps/api/src/index.ts` running on port 3001
- Types at `@rearden/types` with: Candidate (id, email, name, role:"candidate", resume, skills, experience, videoUrl, thumbnailUrl, location, title, bio, availability), ApiResponse<T>, SearchQuery, SearchFilters, SearchResult, SearchResponse

Current app.ts:
```typescript
import { Hono } from "hono";
import { logger } from "hono/logger";
import { cors } from "hono/cors";
import type { ApiResponse } from "@rearden/types";

export const app = new Hono();

app.use("*", logger());
app.use("*", cors());

app.get("/", (c) => {
  return c.json<ApiResponse>({
    success: true,
    data: { message: "Rearden API" },
  });
});

app.get("/health", (c) => {
  return c.json<ApiResponse>({
    success: true,
    data: { status: "ok", timestamp: new Date().toISOString() },
  });
});
```

## What to Build

### 1. `apps/api/src/data/mockCandidates.ts`
Create 12 diverse mock candidates with realistic data. Each candidate needs:
- Unique UUID-style id (use hardcoded UUIDs)
- Realistic name, email, title, location, bio
- Real tech skills (3-6 per candidate)
- experience: 1-15 years
- availability: "immediate" | "2weeks" | "1month" | "3months"  
- videoUrl: use these Pexels video file URLs (one per candidate):
  1. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3252118/3252118-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  2. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209828/3209828-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  3. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3255275/3255275-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  4. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065924/4065924-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  5. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3251806/3251806-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  6. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3196487/3196487-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  7. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209298/3209298-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  8. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3195440/3195440-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  9. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4066905/4066905-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  10. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065388/4065388-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  11. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3209191/3209191-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
  12. https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065564/4065564-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4
- thumbnailUrl: `https://images.pexels.com/videos/{videoId}/free-video-{videoId}.jpg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=400` (extract videoId from the URL above)
- role: "candidate" as const
- Export as `export const mockCandidates: Candidate[]`

### 2. `apps/api/src/lib/searchEngine.ts`
```typescript
import type { Candidate, SearchFilters, SearchResult } from "@rearden/types";

export interface ScoredCandidate {
  candidate: Candidate;
  score: number;
  matchReason: string;
}

export function searchCandidates(candidates: Candidate[], query: string, filters?: SearchFilters): ScoredCandidate[]
```
Algorithm:
- Parse query into tokens (lowercase, split by space)
- Score each candidate:
  - Skill exact match: +25pts per skill
  - Skill partial match (token is substring of skill): +15pts per skill
  - Title match (token found in title): +20pts per token
  - Location match (token found in location): +15pts per token
  - Bio match (token found in bio): +5pts per token
  - Experience keywords ("senior" → exp >= 5, "junior" → exp <= 3, "lead" → exp >= 8): +10pts
- Apply filters if provided (skills[], location, experienceMin, experienceMax, availability[])
- Normalize scores to 0-100 scale (max possible points = sum of all match categories for that query)
- Build matchReason string (e.g. "Skills: React, TypeScript | Location: San Francisco")
- Sort descending by score
- Empty query → all candidates with random score 85-100, matchReason: "Featured Talent"
- Filter out candidates with score 0 when query is non-empty

### 3. `apps/api/src/routes/candidates.ts`
Create a Hono router:
```typescript
import { Hono } from "hono";
```
- GET `/` → return all candidates, wrapped in ApiResponse
- GET `/:id` → return single candidate by id, 404 if not found
- POST `/` → accept JSON body, create new candidate (push to in-memory array with generated id), return created candidate

### 4. `apps/api/src/routes/search.ts`
Create a Hono router:
- POST `/` → accepts `{ query: string, filters?: SearchFilters }` body
- Runs searchCandidates with 500ms artificial delay (setTimeout wrapped in Promise)
- Returns ApiResponse with this shape:
```typescript
{
  success: true,
  data: {
    results: scoredCandidates.map(sc => ({
      candidateId: sc.candidate.id,
      score: sc.score,
      matchReason: sc.matchReason,
      candidate: sc.candidate,
    })),
    query,
    totalCandidates: candidates.length,
    searchTimeMs: elapsed,
  }
}
```

### 5. `apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts`
Create a Hono router:
- POST `/` → accept multipart file upload using Hono's built-in body parsing, save to `apps/api/uploads/` directory, return `{ url: "/api/upload/{filename}", filename }`
- GET `/:filename` → serve the uploaded file with proper content-type headers

Use Node.js built-in `fs` and `path`. Create uploads dir if it doesn't exist.

### 6. Update `apps/api/src/app.ts`
Import and wire all routes:
```typescript
import { candidateRoutes } from "./routes/candidates";
import { searchRoutes } from "./routes/search";
import { uploadRoutes } from "./routes/upload";

app.route("/api/candidates", candidateRoutes);
app.route("/api/search", searchRoutes);
app.route("/api/upload", uploadRoutes);
```
Keep existing `/` and `/health` routes.

## Rules
- Use `import type { ... } from "@rearden/types"` for type imports
- Every response uses `c.json<ApiResponse<T>>()` wrapper pattern
- Hono v4 patterns: `c.json()`, `c.req.json()`, `c.req.param("id")`
- For multipart form data in Hono: `c.req.parseBody()` 
- Do NOT install any new packages
- Write ALL files completely

content.text: All 4 tasks pass — type checks and builds for both API and web. Let me also quickly check that the API index doesn't have import path issues with `.js` extensions:
Everything looks good. Let me now do a quick smoke test by starting the dev servers:

sleep 8
# Check if both servers are up
curl -s http://localhost:3001/api/candidates | head -c 200
echo ""
echo "---"
curl -s http://localhost:3001/health | head -c 100

message.content:
C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden>claude
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Settings Error

 C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\.claude\settings.local.json
  └ permissions
    └ allow
      ├ "mcp__(
      │ └ *)": Tool names must start with uppercase. Use "Mcp__"
      ├ "WebFetch(
      │ └ *)": WebFetch permissions must use "domain:" prefix. Use "domain:hostname" format. Examples: WebFetch(domain:example.com),
  WebFetch(domain:*.google.com)
      └ "WebSearch(
        └ *)": WebSearch does not support wildcards. Use exact search terms without * or ?. Examples: WebSearch(claude ai), WebSearch(typescript tutorial)

 Files with errors are skipped entirely, not just the invalid settings. что это за хуйня? Есть инфа?

content.thinking:
The user is getting settings errors from Claude Code about their `.claude/settings.local.json` file. The error messages are clear - there are three issues:

1. `mcp__(*)` - Tool names must start with uppercase, should be `Mcp__`
2. `WebFetch(*)` - WebFetch permissions need `domain:` prefix
3. `WebSearch(*)` - WebSearch doesn't support wildcards

Let me look at the settings file to fix it.

content.text: Это Claude Code ужесточил валидацию permissions. Давай гляну файл и пофикшу.

file.content:
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(.*)",
      "Read(.*)",
      "Write(.*)",
      "Edit(.*)",
      "WebFetch(.*)",
      "WebSearch(.*)",
      "Glob(.*)",
      "Grep(.*)",
      "NotebookEdit(.*)",
      "Task(.*)",
      "mcp__(.*)"
    ],
    "deny": []
  }
}

content.text:
Три проблемы:
1. `mcp__(.*)` — должно быть с большой буквы `Mcp__`
2. `WebFetch(.*)` — нужен формат `domain:*`
3. `WebSearch(.*)` — не поддерживает wildcard

content.content: The file C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\.claude\settings.local.json has been overwritten successfully.

content.text:
Убрал `WebFetch(.*)` и `WebSearch(.*)` (они теперь не принимают wildcard — будут просить разрешение при каждом вызове), и поправил `mcp__` → `Mcp__`.

Если хочешь разрешить WebFetch для конкретных доменов, можно добавить вот так:
```
"WebFetch(domain:*.pexels.com)",
"WebFetch(domain:fonts.googleapis.com)"
```

WebSearch теперь вообще не поддерживает wildcards — только конкретные термины или без ограничений (просто не добавлять в allow, тогда будет спрашивать).

content.content:
> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Configure permissions

> Control what Claude Code can access and do with fine-grained permission rules, modes, and managed policies.

Claude Code supports fine-grained permissions so that you can specify exactly what the agent is allowed to do and what it cannot. Permission settings can be checked into version control and distributed to all developers in your organization, as well as customized by individual developers.

## Permission system

Claude Code uses a tiered permission system to balance power and safety:

| Tool type         | Example          | Approval required | "Yes, don't ask again" behavior               |
| :---------------- | :--------------- | :---------------- | :-------------------------------------------- |
| Read-only         | File reads, Grep | No                | N/A                                           |
| Bash commands     | Shell execution  | Yes               | Permanently per project directory and command |
| File modification | Edit/write files | Yes               | Until session end                             |

## Manage permissions

You can view and manage Claude Code's tool permissions with `/permissions`. This UI lists all permission rules and the settings.json file they are sourced from.

* **Allow** rules let Claude Code use the specified tool without manual approval.
* **Ask** rules prompt for confirmation whenever Claude Code tries to use the specified tool.
* **Deny** rules prevent Claude Code from using the specified tool.

Rules are evaluated in order: **deny -> ask -> allow**. The first matching rule wins, so deny rules always take precedence.

## Permission modes

Claude Code supports several permission modes that control how tools are approved. Set the `defaultMode` in your [settings files](/en/settings#settings-files):

| Mode                | Description                                                                             |
| :------------------ | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `default`           | Standard behavior: prompts for permission on first use of each tool                     |
| `acceptEdits`       | Automatically accepts file edit permissions for the session                             |
| `plan`              | Plan Mode: Claude can analyze but not modify files or execute commands                  |
| `dontAsk`           | Auto-denies tools unless pre-approved via `/permissions` or `permissions.allow` rules   |
| `bypassPermissions` | Skips permission prompts except for writes to protected directories (see warning below) |

<Warning>
  `bypassPermissions` mode skips permission prompts. Writes to `.git`, `.claude`, `.vscode`, and `.idea` directories still prompt for confirmation to prevent accidental corruption of repository state and local configuration. Writes to `.claude/commands`, `.claude/agents`, and `.claude/skills` are exempt and do not prompt, because Claude routinely writes there when creating skills, subagents, and commands. Only use this mode in isolated environments like containers or VMs where Claude Code cannot cause damage. Administrators can prevent this mode by setting `disableBypassPermissionsMode` to `"disable"` in [managed settings](#managed-settings).
</Warning>

## Permission rule syntax

Permission rules follow the format `Tool` or `Tool(specifier)`.

### Match all uses of a tool

To match all uses of a tool, use just the tool name without parentheses:

| Rule       | Effect                         |
| :--------- | :----------------------------- |
| `Bash`     | Matches all Bash commands      |
| `WebFetch` | Matches all web fetch requests |
| `Read`     | Matches all file reads         |

`Bash(*)` is equivalent to `Bash` and matches all Bash commands.

### Use specifiers for fine-grained control

Add a specifier in parentheses to match specific tool uses:

| Rule                           | Effect                                                   |
| :----------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Bash(npm run build)`          | Matches the exact command `npm run build`                |
| `Read(./.env)`                 | Matches reading the `.env` file in the current directory |
| `WebFetch(domain:example.com)` | Matches fetch requests to example.com                    |

### Wildcard patterns

Bash rules support glob patterns with `*`. Wildcards can appear at any position in the command. This configuration allows npm and git commit commands while blocking git push:

```json  theme={null}
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(npm run *)",
      "Bash(git commit *)",
      "Bash(git * main)",
      "Bash(* --version)",
      "Bash(* --help *)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Bash(git push *)"
    ]
  }
}
```

The space before `*` matters: `Bash(ls *)` matches `ls -la` but not `lsof`, while `Bash(ls*)` matches both. The legacy `:*` suffix syntax is equivalent to ` *` but is deprecated.

## Tool-specific permission rules

### Bash

Bash permission rules support wildcard matching with `*`. Wildcards can appear at any position in the command, including at the beginning, middle, or end:

* `Bash(npm run build)` matches the exact Bash command `npm run build`
* `Bash(npm run test *)` matches Bash commands starting with `npm run test`
* `Bash(npm *)` matches any command starting with `npm `
* `Bash(* install)` matches any command ending with ` install`
* `Bash(git * main)` matches commands like `git checkout main`, `git merge main`

When `*` appears at the end with a space before it (like `Bash(ls *)`), it enforces a word boundary, requiring the prefix to be followed by a space or end-of-string. For example, `Bash(ls *)` matches `ls -la` but not `lsof`. In contrast, `Bash(ls*)` without a space matches both `ls -la` and `lsof` because there's no word boundary constraint.

<Tip>
  Claude Code is aware of shell operators (like `&&`) so a prefix match rule like `Bash(safe-cmd *)` won't give it permission to run the command `safe-cmd && other-cmd`.
</Tip>

When you approve a compound command with "Yes, don't ask again", Claude Code saves a separate rule for each subcommand that requires approval, rather than a single rule for the full compound string. For example, approving `git status && npm test` saves a rule for `npm test`, so future `npm test` invocations are recognized regardless of what precedes the `&&`. Subcommands like `cd` into a subdirectory generate their own Read rule for that path. Up to 5 rules may be saved for a single compound command.

<Warning>
  Bash permission patterns that try to constrain command arguments are fragile. For example, `Bash(curl http://github.com/ *)` intends to restrict curl to GitHub URLs, but won't match variations like:

  * Options before URL: `curl -X GET http://github.com/...`
  * Different protocol: `curl https://github.com/...`
  * Redirects: `curl -L http://bit.ly/xyz` (redirects to github)
  * Variables: `URL=http://github.com && curl $URL`
  * Extra spaces: `curl  http://github.com`

  For more reliable URL filtering, consider:

  * **Restrict Bash network tools**: use deny rules to block `curl`, `wget`, and similar commands, then use the WebFetch tool with `WebFetch(domain:github.com)` permission for allowed domains
  * **Use PreToolUse hooks**: implement a hook that validates URLs in Bash commands and blocks disallowed domains
  * Instructing Claude Code about your allowed curl patterns via CLAUDE.md

  Note that using WebFetch alone does not prevent network access. If Bash is allowed, Claude can still use `curl`, `wget`, or other tools to reach any URL.
</Warning>

### Read and Edit

`Edit` rules apply to all built-in tools that edit files. Claude makes a best-effort attempt to apply `Read` rules to all built-in tools that read files like Grep and Glob.

<Warning>
  Read and Edit deny rules apply to Claude's built-in file tools, not to Bash subprocesses. A `Read(./.env)` deny rule blocks the Read tool but does not prevent `cat .env` in Bash. For OS-level enforcement that blocks all processes from accessing a path, [enable the sandbox](/en/sandboxing).
</Warning>

Read and Edit rules both follow the [gitignore](https://git-scm.com/docs/gitignore) specification with four distinct pattern types:

| Pattern            | Meaning                                | Example                          | Matches                        |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `//path`           | **Absolute** path from filesystem root | `Read(//Users/alice/secrets/**)` | `/Users/alice/secrets/**`      |
| `~/path`           | Path from **home** directory           | `Read(~/Documents/*.pdf)`        | `/Users/alice/Documents/*.pdf` |
| `/path`            | Path **relative to project root**      | `Edit(/src/**/*.ts)`             | `<project root>/src/**/*.ts`   |
| `path` or `./path` | Path **relative to current directory** | `Read(*.env)`                    | `<cwd>/*.env`                  |

<Warning>
  A pattern like `/Users/alice/file` is NOT an absolute path. It's relative to the project root. Use `//Users/alice/file` for absolute paths.
</Warning>

On Windows, paths are normalized to POSIX form before matching. `C:\Users\alice` becomes `/c/Users/alice`, so use `//c/**/.env` to match `.env` files anywhere on that drive. To match across all drives, use `//**/.env`.

Examples:

* `Edit(/docs/**)`: edits in `<project>/docs/` (NOT `/docs/` and NOT `<project>/.claude/docs/`)
* `Read(~/.zshrc)`: reads your home directory's `.zshrc`
* `Edit(//tmp/scratch.txt)`: edits the absolute path `/tmp/scratch.txt`
* `Read(src/**)`: reads from `<current-directory>/src/`

<Note>
  In gitignore patterns, `*` matches files in a single directory while `**` matches recursively across directories. To allow all file access, use just the tool name without parentheses: `Read`, `Edit`, or `Write`.
</Note>

### WebFetch

* `WebFetch(domain:example.com)` matches fetch requests to example.com

### MCP

* `mcp__puppeteer` matches any tool provided by the `puppeteer` server (name configured in Claude Code)
* `mcp__puppeteer__*` wildcard syntax that also matches all tools from the `puppeteer` server
* `mcp__puppeteer__puppeteer_navigate` matches the `puppeteer_navigate` tool provided by the `puppeteer` server

### Agent (subagents)

Use `Agent(AgentName)` rules to control which [subagents](/en/sub-agents) Claude can use:

* `Agent(Explore)` matches the Explore subagent
* `Agent(Plan)` matches the Plan subagent
* `Agent(my-custom-agent)` matches a custom subagent named `my-custom-agent`

Add these rules to the `deny` array in your settings or use the `--disallowedTools` CLI flag to disable specific agents. To disable the Explore agent:

```json  theme={null}
{
  "permissions": {
    "deny": ["Agent(Explore)"]
  }
}
```

## Extend permissions with hooks

[Claude Code hooks](/en/hooks-guide) provide a way to register custom shell commands to perform permission evaluation at runtime. When Claude Code makes a tool call, PreToolUse hooks run before the permission prompt. The hook output can deny the tool call, force a prompt, or skip the prompt to let the call proceed.

Skipping the prompt does not bypass permission rules. Deny and ask rules are still evaluated after a hook returns `"allow"`, so a matching deny rule still blocks the call. This preserves the deny-first precedence described in [Manage permissions](#manage-permissions), including deny rules set in managed settings.

## Working directories

By default, Claude has access to files in the directory where it was launched. You can extend this access:

* **During startup**: use `--add-dir <path>` CLI argument
* **During session**: use `/add-dir` command
* **Persistent configuration**: add to `additionalDirectories` in [settings files](/en/settings#settings-files)

Files in additional directories follow the same permission rules as the original working directory: they become readable without prompts, and file editing permissions follow the current permission mode.

## How permissions interact with sandboxing

Permissions and [sandboxing](/en/sandboxing) are complementary security layers:

* **Permissions** control which tools Claude Code can use and which files or domains it can access. They apply to all tools (Bash, Read, Edit, WebFetch, MCP, and others).
* **Sandboxing** provides OS-level enforcement that restricts the Bash tool's filesystem and network access. It applies only to Bash commands and their child processes.

Use both for defense-in-depth:

* Permission deny rules block Claude from even attempting to access restricted resources
* Sandbox restrictions prevent Bash commands from reaching resources outside defined boundaries, even if a prompt injection bypasses Claude's decision-making
* Filesystem restrictions in the sandbox use Read and Edit deny rules, not separate sandbox configuration
* Network restrictions combine WebFetch permission rules with the sandbox's `allowedDomains` list

## Managed settings

For organizations that need centralized control over Claude Code configuration, administrators can deploy managed settings that cannot be overridden by user or project settings. These policy settings follow the same format as regular settings files and can be delivered through MDM/OS-level policies, managed settings files, or [server-managed settings](/en/server-managed-settings). See [settings files](/en/settings#settings-files) for delivery mechanisms and file locations.

### Managed-only settings

Some settings are only effective in managed settings:

| Setting                                        | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| :--------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `disableBypassPermissionsMode`                 | Set to `"disable"` to prevent `bypassPermissions` mode and the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag                                                                                                                                 |
| `allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly`              | When `true`, prevents user and project settings from defining `allow`, `ask`, or `deny` permission rules. Only rules in managed settings apply                                                                                       |
| `allowManagedHooksOnly`                        | When `true`, prevents loading of user, project, and plugin hooks. Only managed hooks and SDK hooks are allowed                                                                                                                       |
| `allowManagedMcpServersOnly`                   | When `true`, only `allowedMcpServers` from managed settings are respected. `deniedMcpServers` still merges from all sources. See [Managed MCP configuration](/en/mcp#managed-mcp-configuration)                                      |
| `blockedMarketplaces`                          | Blocklist of marketplace sources. Blocked sources are checked before downloading, so they never touch the filesystem. See [managed marketplace restrictions](/en/plugin-marketplaces#managed-marketplace-restrictions)               |
| `sandbox.network.allowManagedDomainsOnly`      | When `true`, only `allowedDomains` and `WebFetch(domain:...)` allow rules from managed settings are respected. Non-allowed domains are blocked automatically without prompting the user. Denied domains still merge from all sources |
| `sandbox.filesystem.allowManagedReadPathsOnly` | When `true`, only `allowRead` paths from managed settings are respected. `allowRead` entries from user, project, and local settings are ignored                                                                                      |
| `strictKnownMarketplaces`                      | Controls which plugin marketplaces users can add. See [managed marketplace restrictions](/en/plugin-marketplaces#managed-marketplace-restrictions)                                                                                   |

<Note>
  Access to [Remote Control](/en/remote-control) and [web sessions](/en/claude-code-on-the-web) is not controlled by a managed settings key. On Team and Enterprise plans, an admin enables or disables these features in [Claude Code admin settings](https://claude.ai/admin-settings/claude-code).
</Note>

## Settings precedence

Permission rules follow the same [settings precedence](/en/settings#settings-precedence) as all other Claude Code settings:

1. **Managed settings**: cannot be overridden by any other level, including command line arguments
2. **Command line arguments**: temporary session overrides
3. **Local project settings** (`.claude/settings.local.json`)
4. **Shared project settings** (`.claude/settings.json`)
5. **User settings** (`~/.claude/settings.json`)

If a tool is denied at any level, no other level can allow it. For example, a managed settings deny cannot be overridden by `--allowedTools`, and `--disallowedTools` can add restrictions beyond what managed settings define.

If a permission is allowed in user settings but denied in project settings, the project setting takes precedence and the permission is blocked.

## Example configurations

This [repository](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/examples/settings) includes starter settings configurations for common deployment scenarios. Use these as starting points and adjust them to fit your needs.

## See also

* [Settings](/en/settings): complete configuration reference including the permission settings table
* [Sandboxing](/en/sandboxing): OS-level filesystem and network isolation for Bash commands
* [Authentication](/en/authentication): set up user access to Claude Code
* [Security](/en/security): security safeguards and best practices
* [Hooks](/en/hooks-guide): automate workflows and extend permission evaluation
